sourcePrompt: An M16A1, the standard US infantry rifle of the Vietnam War, about a meter long and lying level. A slim straight-line rifle: a triangular front sight tower above the muzzle, a fixed carry handle housing the rear sight, ribbed cylindrical handguards around a thin barrel, a magazine curving slightly forward below the receiver, a pistol grip, and a solid. Low-poly, game-ready, clean hard edges.
